skill.name: debugging
skill.description: Systematically debug code issues using proven methodologies. Use when encountering errors, unexpected behavior, or performance problems. Handles error analysis, root cause identification, debugging strategies, and fix verification.
skill.summary: Guide systematic debugging from error evidence through root cause analysis and verified fixes.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged command examples, reference URLs, prose matches, and debugging questions. Review found these are false positives in documentation, with no executable scripts, data exfiltration, prompt injection, or malicious intent.

dangerous_patterns.title: Command Examples in Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged external command locations are fenced examples and debugging snippets. They do not define executable skill code or dynamic command construction.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The matches appear inside Markdown code fences or example sections. No file contains runnable shell logic, interpolation, or user-controlled command execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Reference URLs in Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The hardcoded URLs are educational references in the References section. They do not transmit data or instruct automated network access.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are static Markdown links to debugging resources. There is no request code, credential handling, or outbound data flow.

dangerous_patterns.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern False Positive
dangerous_patterns.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged locations are YAML frontmatter and plain instructional text. No cryptographic algorithm or hashing implementation is present.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Review of the exact lines shows descriptive text, not weak cryptographic code. The scanner appears to have matched text fragments.

dangerous_patterns.title: System Reconnaissance Pattern False Positive
dangerous_patterns.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged lines are normal debugging context gathering and a Python dictionary example. They do not probe host state or collect secrets.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Line 31 asks when a bug started, and line 127 checks a sample user dictionary. Neither line requests system inventory or sensitive environment data.

content.value_statement: Bugs often waste time when teams chase symptoms instead of evidence. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through reproducible debugging steps.
actual_capabilities: Collect error messages, stack traces, environment details, and recent changes before proposing fixes.
Guide users toward minimal reproduction steps and focused test cases.
Apply isolation methods such as logging, divide and conquer, and binary search debugging.
Identify common patterns including null references, race conditions, memory leaks, and type mismatches.
Recommend fixes that address root causes and add regression coverage.
Suggest language-specific debuggers and profilers listed in the skill documentation.
limitations: Requires users to provide relevant code, logs, errors, or reproduction details.
Cannot directly inspect systems, databases, or production environments without user-provided evidence.
May need repeated observations for intermittent bugs and timing issues.
Does not replace security, incident response, or performance engineering review for high-risk systems.

use_cases.title: Resolve Runtime Exceptions
use_cases.description: Use stack traces and failing inputs to identify why a function crashes and how to handle the failing case.

use_cases.title: Stabilize Flaky Tests
use_cases.description: Investigate intermittent CI failures by checking shared state, timing assumptions, and missing synchronization.

use_cases.title: Investigate Performance Regressions
use_cases.description: Compare recent changes, collect profiler evidence, and narrow the cause of slow or memory-heavy behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Explain an Error
prompt_templates.prompt: I see this error: [paste error]. Here is the related code: [paste code]. Explain the likely cause and first fix to try.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner debugging of a clear error message

prompt_templates.title: Create Reproduction Steps
prompt_templates.prompt: This bug happens when [describe behavior]. Help me turn it into minimal reproduction steps and a focused failing test.

prompt_templates.title: Find the Root Cause
prompt_templates.prompt: The bug started after these changes: [list changes]. Here are logs and symptoms: [paste details]. Identify the most likely root cause.

prompt_templates.title: Debug an Intermittent Failure
prompt_templates.prompt: This test fails only sometimes in CI. Here is the test, related code, and timing information: [paste details]. Build a debugging plan.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced analysis for race conditions or order dependencies

output_examples.input: A function crashes when the user ID does not exist.
output_examples.output: The likely cause is missing handling for the not-found path. Add explicit handling, then write a regression test for a missing user ID.

output_examples.input: A CI test passes locally but fails intermittently.
output_examples.output: Start by checking shared mutable state, timing assumptions, and test order dependencies. Add targeted logging before changing implementation code.

output_examples.input: Memory usage grows while processing a large dataset.
output_examples.output: Profile the loop and inspect retained objects. Consider streaming results, releasing resources, or replacing unbounded lists with generators.

best_practices: Reproduce the issue before changing code.
Make one focused change, then verify the result.
Add a regression test for every confirmed bug fix.
anti_patterns: Changing several possible causes at the same time.
Suppressing errors without understanding the failure.
Ignoring stack traces, logs, or recent code changes.

faq.question: What should I provide for the best debugging result?
faq.answer: Provide the full error, stack trace, related code, expected behavior, actual behavior, environment details, and recent changes.

faq.question: Can this skill debug performance problems?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide profiler selection, compare recent changes, and identify likely bottlenecks from evidence you provide.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It documents example commands and debugging workflows, but the skill package does not include executable scripts.

faq.question: Can it help with intermittent bugs?
faq.answer: Yes. It suggests isolation steps for timing, race conditions, shared state, and flaky test order dependencies.

faq.question: Will it fix code without understanding the root cause?
faq.answer: No. The skill emphasizes reproducing the issue, isolating the cause, and verifying the fix with tests.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
